name: Directus MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Directus — the open-source headless CMS and data platform. From rijkvanzanten, the creator of Directus. Enables AI agents to interact with Directus collections, items, users, files, and settings through Directus's REST and GraphQL API — supporting AI-driven content management and data workflows on Directus deployments.
use_cases: Managing Directus content collections and items from content automation agents
Querying and updating database records through Directus's abstracted API
Managing files, users, and permissions from data governance agents
Building AI-powered content pipelines on Directus as the data layer
not_for: Teams using other CMS platforms (WordPress, Contentful, Sanity, etc.)
Projects not using Directus as their data platform
Direct database access without Directus API abstraction
best_when: A team using Directus as their data platform or headless CMS wants AI agents to manage content, query data, and interact with their Directus collections — from Directus's own creator.
avoid_when: You use other CMS/data platforms. This is Directus-specific. rijkvanzanten is the Directus creator — this is essentially an official implementation.

auth.notes: Directus static token or user token required. Generated in Directus admin panel. Tokens inherit user permissions — use dedicated service accounts with minimal permissions for agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Directus is free self-hosted (BUSL license for core, MIT for SDKs). Directus Cloud has paid tiers.
pricing.notes: Directus can be self-hosted for free. MCP server from the creator is free open source.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no rate limits; Directus Cloud limits by plan

known_agent_gotchas: Collection schema is project-specific — agents need schema introspection first
Directus permissions model is rich — use dedicated service users with minimal collection access
rijkvanzanten is the Directus creator — this implementation has excellent quality signal
Directus's flexible permissions can expose unintended data — configure service account carefully
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS to Directus instance. Token-based auth. Directus manages multi-tenant data — proper permission configuration is critical. Service account tokens should have minimal required permissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: Creator-built implementation. Token authentication. Permission model requires careful configuration. GDPR compliant when self-hosted.
